mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline in more than 70% of co-occurring months, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term equity momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of recovery-phase rebounds, or return autocorrelation completely dominates the macro signal predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and compresses equity multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive returns dominate when both inflation and rate conditions are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which sustains consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real gross domestic product confirms recessionary conditions, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 40% of quarters following a negative GDP print, or the signal fails to separate recessionary from expansionary return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial output and contraction in the unemployment rate signals healthy labor-demand synchronization, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate models, or forward returns are negative in more than 60% of synchronized periods.
